01 — Exploratory Data Analysis

Decision Distribution
Comparison

6 questions compared side-by-side — Human (blue) vs AI (orange) response counts across decision categories: logical, social-influence, emotional, ethical, risk-taking, experience-based.

Chart 01
Decision Distribution Comparison: Human vs AI
Q1, Q7, Q14, Q21, Q28, Q31 — each subplot shows how humans and AI distributed answers. Human responses show wider spread; AI clusters on logical and ethical categories.
Q1: Humans favour logical (18), AI favour ethical (9) Q7: Both peak at logical; AI more dispersed Q14: Humans strongly logical (32), AI social-influence (17) Q28 & Q31 show highest AI-human divergence

02 — Radar Analysis

Reasoning Profile

Comparing AI and human performance across five core decision-making dimensions simultaneously. Humans lead on ethics and emotion; AI leads on consistency and logic.

Chart 02
Radar Chart — Multi-Dimensional Comparison
Humans outperform AI in empathy, ethics, and experience-based reasoning. AI outperforms in consistency and speed.
Humans lead: Ethics (81) · Emotion (87) · Experience (79) AI leads: Logic (88) · Consistency (91)

03 — Correlation Analysis

Scatter Matrix

Decision Point Correlations between Q1–Q5. Pink = Human, Teal = AI. Diagonal shows KDE distribution curves. AI clusters tightly; humans spread widely.

Chart 03
Scatter Matrix: Decision Point Correlations (Human vs AI)
A 5×5 pairplot matrix showing how each question pair correlates. AI responses (teal) cluster more tightly around specific values; human responses (pink) spread wider, reflecting personal variation and emotional influence.
AI (teal) clusters tightly — high consistency pattern Human (pink) spreads widely — personal variation Q2 shows sharpest AI vs human divergence

04 — Machine Learning

Model Performance
& Confusion Matrix

Three classifiers trained to distinguish AI from human responses. XGBoost achieved the strongest separation with 85% accuracy.

Chart 04a
Classifier Accuracy & F1 Score
XGBoost outperforms all other classifiers. Bars animate on scroll.
Logistic RegressionBaseline
Accuracy 72% · F1 Score 69%
Random ForestImproved
Accuracy 79% · F1 Score 76%
XGBoost✦ Best
Accuracy 85% · F1 Score 83%
XGBoost: best feature learning + consistency Random Forest: reduced overfitting vs baseline

05 — Explainable AI

SHAP Feature Importance
— All 31 Questions

SHapley Additive Explanations reveal which questions most strongly distinguish AI from human responses. Colour gradient: purple (high) → yellow (low) impact.

Chart 05
Global Feature Importance: Ranking of All 31 Questions
Q20 is the strongest predictor (impact ≈ 0.16), followed by Q17 (0.14) and Q16 (0.09). Ethically sensitive and emotionally complex questions dominate the top rankings — AI and humans diverge most sharply on these scenarios.
Q20 · Q17 — Top predictors (ethical/emotional) Q16 · Q2 · Q30 — High importance cluster Q19 — Lowest discriminating power Moderate → strong effect sizes on ethics questions

06 — Key Findings

What the Data Reveals

Core insights from statistical testing, ML classification, and SHAP analysis.

⚡
AI is fast and consistent
AI responses are more pattern-driven and consistent — clustering on logical and ethical categories with low variability.
💡
Humans lead on ethics & empathy
Human responses show stronger emotional intelligence, ethical reasoning, and experience-based judgment. Cohen's d shows moderate-to-strong effect sizes.
🔬
Statistical significance confirmed
T-Test and Chi-Square showed significant differences across multiple questions. Humans and AI approach decisions differently.
🏆
XGBoost best classifier
85% accuracy — XGBoost achieved the strongest separation between AI and human responses, outperforming all other models.
⚖️
Ethical questions most discriminating
SHAP confirms Q20, Q17, Q16, and Q2 have the highest feature importance — ethics separates AI from humans most clearly.
🤝
Hybrid model recommended
AI handles data processing and predictions. Humans supervise ethical and sensitive decisions. Neither replaces the other.
🤖 AI — Strengths
Faster processing speed
Consistent reproducible responses
Efficient large dataset analysis
Reduced manual workload
🤖 AI — Weaknesses
Limited emotional understanding
Training data dependency
Algorithmic bias risk
Lacks deep contextual reasoning
🧑 Human — Strengths
Ethical reasoning & empathy
Emotional intelligence
Creativity and adaptability
Contextual understanding
🧑 Human — Weaknesses
Emotional bias in decisions
Slower processing speed
Inconsistency in repetition
Fatigue and cognitive overload

07 — Conclusion

The Verdict

AI can imitate certain human decision patterns — particularly in logical scenarios — but human decisions remain more emotional, ethical, and experience-driven, while AI decisions are more consistent and data-driven. AI should support — not replace — human judgment, especially in ethically sensitive contexts.

Responsible AI implementation requires ethical governance, legal compliance (GDPR · PECA), transparency through explainable AI, and continuous human oversight aligned with IEEE, ACM, and BCS professional standards.

Department of Computer Science · Fatima Jinnah Women University
